examples.title: Demonstrating CPSR Flags
examples.description: ARM assembly code showing how different instructions affect CPSR flags
examples.code:
; CPSR Flag Demonstration Program
; Initialize registers
 MOV R0, #10 ; R0 = 10
 MOV R1, #5 ; R1 = 5
 
; Addition - affects C and V flags
 ADD R2, R0, R1 ; R2 = 15, C=0 (no carry), V=0 (no overflow)
 
; Subtraction - affects C and V flags
 SUB R3, R0, R1 ; R3 = 5, Z=0 (result non-zero), N=0 (positive)
 
; Compare instruction - only affects flags
 CMP R0, R1 ; 10 - 5 = 5, Z=0, N=0, C=1 (no borrow)
 
; Conditional branch using flags
 BEQ equal ; Branch if Z=1 (not taken here)
 BGT greater ; Branch if Z=0 and N=V (taken: 10>5)
 
greater:
 MOV R4, #1 ; Mark as greater
 B end
 
equal:
 MOV R4, #0 ; Mark as equal
 
end:
 ; Test zero flag
 MOV R5, #0 ; R5 = 0, Z will be set
 CMP R5, #0 ; Compare: 0-0=0, Z=1 (set)
 BEQ zero_val ; Branch taken since Z=1
 
zero_val:
 MOV R6, #100 ; This executes
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how arithmetic and logical instructions modify the CPSR flags. ADD/SUB affect C and V, CMP only changes flags without storing result, and conditional branches (BEQ, BGT) depend on flag states.

examples.title: Exception Handler Example
examples.description: Code showing exception handling and CPSR/SPSR usage
examples.code:
; Exception Handler Example for IRQ

; Vector Table (at address 0x00000000)
 B start ; Reset vector
 B undefined_handler ; Undefined instruction
 B swi_handler ; SWI handler
 B prefetch_abort ; Prefetch abort
 B data_abort ; Data abort 
 B . ; Reserved
 B irq_handler ; IRQ vector
 B fiq_handler ; FIQ vector

start:
 LDR SP, =0x4000 ; Initialize stack
 BL main ; Branch to main

; IRQ Handler
irq_handler:
 ; Standard IRQ entry sequence
 SUB LR, LR, #4 ; Adjust return address
 
 ; Save registers and CPSR
 PUSH {R0-R12, LR}
 MRS R0, SPSR ; Copy SPSR to register
 PUSH {R0}
 
 ; Change to Supervisor mode to access more registers
 MRS R1, CPSR
 BIC R1, R1, #0x1F ; Clear mode bits
 ORR R1, R1, #0x13 ; Set Supervisor mode
 MSR CPSR_c, R1
 
 ; Call C IRQ handler
 BL irq_handler_c
 
 ; Restore CPSR
 POP {R0}
 MSR SPSR_cxsf, R0
 
 ; Restore registers and return
 POP {R0-R12, PC}^ ; ^ restores CPSR from SPSR

; Dummy C handler
irq_handler_c:
 BX LR
examples.explanation: This shows how CPSR is saved to SPSR during IRQ exception. The handler saves context, uses MRS to read SPSR, and uses MSR with ^ suffix to restore CPSR from SPSR on return.

examples.title: Switching Processor Modes
examples.description: Assembly code demonstrating manual mode switching using CPSR
examples.code:
; Mode Switching Demonstration

; Mode bit values
USER_MODE EQU 0x10
FIQ_MODE EQU 0x11
IRQ_MODE EQU 0x12
SVC_MODE EQU 0x13
ABORT_MODE EQU 0x17
UNDEF_MODE EQU 0x1B
SYS_MODE EQU 0x1F

 AREA |C$$code|, CODE, READONLY
 EXPORT switch_mode_demo

switch_mode_demo PROC
 ; Currently in SVC mode (after reset)
 
 ; Switch to User mode using MSR
 MRS R0, CPSR ; Read current CPSR
 BIC R1, R0, #0x1F ; Clear mode bits (bits 0-4)
 ORR R1, R1, #USER_MODE ; Set User mode
 MSR CPSR_c, R1 ; Write back - now in User mode
 
 ; In User mode - can only read condition flags
 ; Try to modify I bit - will have no effect
 ORR R1, R1, #0x80 ; Try to set I bit
 MSR CPSR_c, R1 ; In User mode, this is ignored
 
 ; Switch back to SVC mode
 MRS R0, CPSR
 BIC R1, R0, #0x1F
 ORR R1, R1, #SVC_MODE
 MSR CPSR_c, R1
 
 ; Now in SVC mode - can modify all bits
 ; Disable FIQ
 MRS R0, CPSR
 ORR R0, R0, #0x40 ; Set F bit to 1
 MSR CPSR_c, R0
 
 ENDP
 END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates mode switching using MSR instruction. Shows that User mode cannot modify control bits (privilege violation), while privileged modes like SVC can modify all CPSR bits including I and F interrupt disable bits.
